title: Full STEAM ahead for new regional school's first cohort of students this fall
summary: A new regional STEAM school is opening in downtown Syracuse this fall in the renovated 125-year-old Central Technical High School building. The inaugural cohort of 250 students (80% from Syracuse city) will occupy the first two floors while construction continues, with plans to eventually grow to 1,000 students over four years. City and county leaders view this as a potential model for cross-district educational collaboration and regional student recruitment.

political_signal.description: Cross-sector leadership (mayor, county executive, school superintendent) publicly championing regional STEAM education initiative as economic development and workforce pipeline strategy; framed as potential statewide model.

processing_notes: Article focuses on K-12 education rather than municipal IT operations; relevance to CIO office is indirect (facilities/construction digital systems, potential future technology integration in school infrastructure)
Historic preservation angle may intersect with accessibility/ADA compliance standards
